codeReview.summary: Solid refactoring that cleanly wires the compositor to the render library's CpuBackend. All 7 old rendering files removed from compositor/, duplicate helpers eliminated, SVG rasterization accessed through render::svg public API, build.rs updated with --extern render and rerun-if-changed directives. The damage-aware rendering API (prepare_frame/finish_frame/is_full_repaint) is well-designed with correct state transitions. Memory safety improved by replacing leaked raw pointer for icon coverage with an owned Vec<u8>. All 1764 tests pass, QEMU visual verification confirms correct rendering. The diff is -4042/+298 lines — a significant cleanup.

issues.description: Dead code: make_ctx() method is defined but never called. The render() trait impl builds RenderCtx inline to work around the borrow checker (can't borrow self immutably for make_ctx while also borrowing self.pool mutably). Either remove make_ctx() or add #[allow(dead_code)] with a comment explaining it's kept for future use.

All CpuBackend fields are pub, needed for direct struct literal construction from compositor. A constructor (CpuBackend::new(...)) would provide better encapsulation and allow the struct fields to be private. Not blocking since this is a no_std bare-metal context where the compositor is the sole consumer, but worth noting as a future cleanup.

First-frame rendering bypasses prepare_frame(), directly calling backend.damage.mark_full_screen() then backend.render(). This works correctly (prepare_frame would also return Full since prev_node_count=0 != actual count), but reaching into backend.damage directly couples the compositor to CpuBackend's internal structure rather than using the prepare_frame API. Consider using backend.prepare_frame() for the first frame too, for consistency.

Compositor reads backend.damage.count directly (line 544) to decide rendering path after prepare_frame(). The FrameAction return value from prepare_frame() already communicates Partial vs Skip, so this secondary check is somewhat redundant. The damage.count check after is_full_repaint() being false and action being non-Skip should always be > 0, since prepare_frame() only returns Partial when damage.count > 0 or damage.full_screen. Minor redundancy, not a bug.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md in .factory/library/ still describes PREV_BOUNDS as a compositor static mut ('The compositor maintains per-node previous-frame physical bounds (PREV_BOUNDS array...)'). After this feature, PREV_BOUNDS is encapsulated inside CpuBackend in the render library. The documentation should be updated to reflect the new ownership model.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md lines 43-47 reference 'PREV_BOUNDS' as compositor state. After commit 1497dc2, prev_bounds is a field of render::CpuBackend (system/libraries/render/lib.rs:81).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The os-worker skill's TDD step (step 2: 'Write Tests First') doesn't account for pure refactoring features where all behavioral tests already exist. The worker correctly skipped it and noted this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. The skill procedure could add a note: 'For refactoring features where existing tests serve as the behavioral specification, TDD step can be skipped — existing passing tests ARE the spec.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The os-worker skill's TDD step (step 2) doesn't apply well to pure wiring/refactoring features where all tests already exist. Could add a note...'

summary: Feature passes review. The compositor is cleanly wired to the render library's CpuBackend. All validation assertions (VAL-LIB-005, VAL-COMP-001, VAL-TEST-001, VAL-BUILD-002, VAL-VIS-001, VAL-CROSS-001) are satisfied: old files removed, CpuBackend used via RenderBackend trait, no duplicate helpers, build.rs updated, 1764 tests pass, QEMU visual verification shows correct rendering. Four non-blocking issues noted: dead make_ctx() method, all-pub fields on CpuBackend, first-frame rendering bypassing prepare_frame(), and minor redundant damage.count check. Two shared state observations: stale PREV_BOUNDS documentation in architecture.md, and os-worker skill TDD step needs a refactoring exception note.
